docs.title: Numen-rpg — wiki
docs.text:
This documentation unifies Lore, Reconstruction, and Mechanics on each page.

Layers

📜 Lore — in-world account (varies by era) 🧪 Reconstruction — modern synthesis (Era IV) ⚙️ Mechanics — hard rules, tests, and tables

Runes are legacy. The active system is mineral- and apparatus-based.

docs.title: Tags
docs.text: List of tags auto-generated by plugin.

docs.title: Bestiary
docs.text: • Fill core species and anomalies.

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text: • Initialized structure on 2025-09-18.

docs.title: Editorial Style
docs.text:
• One page → three layers (Lore / Reconstruction / Mechanics).
  • SI for energy (J); erg is magical capacity only.
  • Keep all formulas in ⚙️ Mechanics.
  • Use tasklists at the top for open questions.

docs.title: Writing Guide
docs.text:
1. Start with a tasklist of questions and TODOs.
  2. Fill 📜 Lore per era (I–IV), then 🧪 Reconstruction, then ⚙️ Mechanics.
  3. Add tags at the bottom, e.g. #era-iv #engineering.

docs.title: Engineering
docs.text: Mineral-based operations and apparatus.

docs.title: Anti-Thermal Weapons
docs.text:
• Effective range (air vs vacuum)
  • Shutdown behavior near 0 K

docs.title: ⚙️ Mechanics
docs.text: • Per-turn ΔT in target zone; failure checks increase as T approaches T_cut. Tags: #engineering #weapons

docs.title: Apparatus
docs.text:
• Standard module interfaces and maintenance checks
  • Schematics: city furnace, thermal sidearm

Tags: #engineering #apparatus

docs.title: Capacitors (Magical Capacity Stores)
docs.text:
• Table: diamond, corundum, quartz (erg/kg)
  • Saturation rules and fatigue

Tags: #engineering #capacitor

docs.title: Catalysts
docs.text:
• Profiles for thermal, electrical, kinetic facilitation
  • Loss coefficients vs temperature

Tags: #engineering #catalyst

docs.title: Converters (Tain → Heat/Electricity)
docs.text:
• Provide linear model near room T, behavior near T_cut
  • Three failure modes with thresholds

docs.title: ⚙️ Mechanics
docs.text:
• Power: Qdot = α · V_mag (scene-scale).

Tags: #engineering #converter

docs.title: Functional Mineralogy
docs.text:
• Classify modules: Converters, Catalysts, Capacitors, Isolators, Channels, Shapers.

Tags: #engineering

docs.title: Isolators
docs.text:
• Designs that shield Tain but retain Vis coupling
  • Do isolators raise local T_cut?

Tags: #engineering #isolator

docs.title: Legacy: Runes
docs.text:
Mapping from classic runes to modern mineral modules.

  • Build table: Rune → Module Set Tags: #legacy #mapping

docs.title: Mother Crystals
docs.text:
• Distinguish stationary vs transportable classes
  • Define control hysteresis and shutdown protocol
  • Model σ_T uplift radius
Layers

📜 Lore — infinite sources revered and feared; cities and wars formed around them. 🧪 Reconstruction — controlled runaway coupling to Tain flows via highly ordered crystals. ⚙️ Mechanics — output, risks, transport.

docs.title: 📜 Lore
docs.text: Mountains of order; portable "ideal mothers" are rare and strategic.

docs.title: 🧪 Reconstruction
docs.text: Runaway is goal, but controlled through isolators and feedback rings.

docs.title: ⚙️ Mechanics
docs.text:
• Nameplate power: MJ/h (indefinite when stable).
  • Failure modes: overrun, lattice burnout, Tain bloom.
  • Transport: Engineering tests per 100 km; on failure roll σ_T event. Tags: #engineering #source

docs.title: Modules
docs.text: Concrete, game-ready building blocks.

docs.title: Combustion Module
docs.text: • Finalize fuel tables and ignition thresholds

docs.title: 📜 Lore
docs.text: From sacred fire to standardized burners.

docs.title: 🧪 Reconstruction
docs.text: Couples fuel (oil) with Vis modulation; no energy from Vis alone.

docs.title: ⚙️ Mechanics
docs.text:
• Modulation: 30 erg per ignition cycle.
  • Fuel: depends on volume; losses scale with flow.
  • Interfaces: quartz channel + thermal catalyst. Tags: #module #combustion

docs.title: 📜 Lore
docs.text: Continuous beams in late eras.

docs.title: 🧪 Reconstruction
docs.text: Sustained output requires stable Ko domain and steady fuel.

docs.title: ⚙️ Mechanics
docs.text:
• Range baseline: 10 m (scene default).
  • Cost: 2 erg/turn + module costs.
  • Accuracy: +1; must maintain contact per turn. Tags: #module #channel

docs.title: 📜 Lore
docs.text: True momentum shots replaced "magic-only" bolts.

docs.title: 🧪 Reconstruction
docs.text: Uses physical momentum (test Shooting + Strength).

docs.title: ⚙️ Mechanics
docs.text:
• Accuracy: +10/+10/+20 by profile.
  • Cost: 1/2/2 erg; projectile mass configurable. Tags: #module #kinetic

docs.title: 📜 Lore
docs.text: From ice barricades to engineered walls.

docs.title: 🧪 Reconstruction
docs.text: Shaper + material selection; T-sensitive stability.

docs.title: ⚙️ Mechanics
docs.text:
• Thickness vs damage threshold table TBD.
  • Cost scales with volume; coherence drops near T_cut. Tags: #module #defense

docs.text: List and link artifacts (include mother crystals).

docs.text: List and link all modules/minerals.

docs.title: Terms Index
docs.text: Glossary of important terms.

docs.title: Metaphysics
docs.text: Vis, Tain, Ko, Matter, Magic Space, Tain Noise.

docs.title: Ko
docs.text:
• Choose baseline radius r_Ko and lifetime τ_Ko
  • Provide temperature dependence of Ko stability

docs.title: 📜 Lore
docs.text: Seeds of magic space.

docs.title: 🧪 Reconstruction
docs.text: Condensation of Vis above density threshold creates Ko domains.

docs.title: ⚙️ Mechanics
docs.text:
• Ko domain radius scales with Vis density and temperature.
  • At near-0 K, Ko coherence → 0. Tags: #metaphysics #ko

docs.title: 📜 Lore
docs.text: Where magic actually happens.

docs.title: 🧪 Reconstruction
docs.text: Requires Ko presence; collapses near 0 K.

docs.title: ⚙️ Mechanics
docs.text: • Operations only valid within Magic Space domains. Tags: #metaphysics #space

docs.title: 📜 Lore
docs.text: Stable mix of Vis and Tain.

docs.title: 🧪 Reconstruction
docs.text: Balance of net-positive Tain reactions and Vis losses.

docs.title: ⚙️ Mechanics
docs.text: • Materials differ by magical capacity (erg/kg) and interfaces. Tags: #metaphysics #matter

docs.title: Tain Noise
docs.text: • Decide σ_T bands and triggers

docs.title: ⚙️ Mechanics
docs.text:
• Low: no extra checks.
  • Medium: complications on failure.
  • High: periodic bloom checks. Tags: #metaphysics #noise

docs.title: Tain
docs.text:
• Define local vs impulsive influx model
  • Calibrate σ_T thresholds (3 bands) and decay over time
  • Add three “Tain bloom” examples with mechanical hooks
Layers

📜 Lore — the destructive substrate, source of chaotic surges. 🧪 Reconstruction — net-positive energy reactions feeding matter persistence; influx from outside the Vis-world. ⚙️ Mechanics — scene-level noise rolls, bloom tables.

docs.title: 📜 Lore
docs.text: Early eras: "black breath" that made magic explode. IV-era: regulated via isolators.

docs.title: 🧪 Reconstruction
docs.text: Tain sustains matter via net-positive reactions, countering Vis losses.

docs.title: ⚙️ Mechanics
docs.text:
• Scene start: roll σ_T (Low/Med/High).
  • On failed operation or High σ_T: trigger Tain Bloom (GM table). Tags: #metaphysics #tain

docs.title: Vis
docs.text:
• Clarify geometry sensitivity (crystal order)
  • Show two counterexamples where Vis alone cannot act (no fuel)
Layers

📜 Lore — order-field overlaying the Tain substrate. 🧪 Reconstruction — Vis modulates transfers; cannot create energy. ⚙️ Mechanics — separate modulation (erg) from fuel (J).

docs.title: 📜 Lore
docs.text: Scholars of II era mistook will for control; minerals were doing the work.

docs.title: 🧪 Reconstruction
docs.text: Vis amplifies transfers and lowers barriers—at a loss—requiring external fuel unless coupled to Tain.

docs.title: ⚙️ Mechanics
docs.text:
• Costs: Modulation (erg) + Fuel (J) + Losses (J).
  • Without fuel or Tain coupling: no macroscopic effect. Tags: #metaphysics #vis

docs.title: Rules
docs.text:
• Use ⚙️ sections for all actionable mechanics.
  • Core load-bearing rules: Units & Measures, Casting, Tests, Stats, Wounds.

docs.title: Casting (Material-Magic Operation)
docs.text:
• Set safe per-turn body draw (erg/turn)
  • Define nośnik (carrier) break probability curve
Layers

📜 Lore — early eras mistook "will" for control; in fact, materials govern magic. 🧪 Reconstruction — operations require configured minerals; thought alone does nothing. ⚙️ Mechanics — costs, tests, failures.

docs.title: Methods
docs.text:
• Direct (body): SelfDamage = max(0, ModulationCost_erg − BodyResilience_erg).
  • Carrier (mineral): Excess = max(0, ModulationCost_erg − CarrierCapacity_erg) P(break) = clamp(p0 + k·Excess, 0, 0.95) with defaults p0=0.1, k=0.05.

docs.title: Fuel
docs.text: • Vis never creates energy; bring fuel (oil/heat/electricity) or couple to Tain sources.

docs.title: Tests
docs.text:
• Operation check: Test(Engineering + Kunszt + situational); complications on failure draw from the Tain Noise table.

Tags: #rules #casting

docs.title: Character Sheet
docs.text:
Checklist of fields and derived stats.

Tags: #rules #sheet

docs.title: Equipment
docs.text:
• Price modular minerals in equipment points
  • Provide 10 sample items for Era II/III/IV

Tags: #rules #equipment

docs.title: Stats
docs.text:
• Add Engineering (Minerals) and Safety (Tain)
  • Define momentum thresholds for knockdown

docs.title: ⚙️ Mechanics
docs.text:
Outline biological vs learned stats and derived defenses (Block vs Dodge).

Tags: #rules #stats

docs.title: Tests
docs.text: • Decide if base die is d120 or variant with explosions

docs.title: ⚙️ Mechanics
docs.text:
• Standard test: roll d120 ≤ Stat (after modifiers).
  • With modifier: Test(Stat + m)
  • Multiple: Test(k·Stat)
  • With threshold: succeed only if roll > T and ≤ Stat.

Tags: #rules #tests

docs.title: Units & Measures
docs.text:
• Confirm full SI adoption for energy/distance
  • Finalize erg as magical capacity (not energy)
Layers

📜 Lore — scholars across eras used mixed units and names. 🧪 Reconstruction — standardize: Joule (J) for energy; erg for magical capacity. ⚙️ Mechanics — conversions and usage.

docs.title: Energy vs Capacity
docs.text:
• Energy: Joule (J), kJ, MJ.
  • Magical capacity: erg (dimensionless in physics; tracks modulation endurance).

docs.title: Conventions
docs.text:
• Costs are split into:
  • Modulation cost (erg) — stress on mineral/apparatus.
  • Fuel cost (J) — energy drawn from chemicals/heat/electricity/Tain.

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
• Ignition of 1 L oil: modulation 30 erg, fuel ~ 35 MJ (scene-scale).
  • Emission-channel beam (3 s): 12 erg modulation + whatever fuel source provides.

Tags: #rules #units

docs.title: Wounds
docs.text:
• Define wound thresholds and accumulation
  • Link Hart/Resilience to BodyResilience_erg

docs.title: ⚙️ Mechanics
docs.text:
Provide wound tiers, bleeding, and shock interactions with low temperature scenes.

Tags: #rules #wounds

docs.title: Era III
docs.text: • Mature engineering, extinction aftermath

docs.title: ⚙️ Mechanics (global)
docs.text: Stability ++, artifacts −, engineering +

docs.title: Era IV
docs.text: • Science era (WW era → space), anti-thermal warfare

docs.title: ⚙️ Mechanics (global)
docs.text: Anti-thermal +++, stable apparatus +++, oceans frozen
